Strong civil liberties orientation (85.38) and high conscientiousness (73.91) drive moderate support, though low trust in judiciary (33.97) and very low system justification (7.06) create some ambivalence about protecting current institutions.

network_context.edge_meaning: The source position is a visible peer whose output was plausibly exposed to the target position; derived from source user liking, reposting, or quoting target user content in the raw data.

peer_exemplars.reasoning: Moderate political trust and high conscientiousness value bureaucratic competence, but very low agreeableness and anti-elitism suspect the civil service is self-serving.
Moderate trust from high agreeableness and authority deference, but tempered by anti-elitism, low external efficacy, and a competitive worldview.

Low external political efficacy (19.45) and strong anti-elitism (60.97) produce slight distrust, though high conscientiousness (73.91) and moderate trust in government (42.77) keep sentiment near neutral.

peer_exemplars.reasoning: High political efficacy and external political trust support basic trust, but populist manichean worldview and collective narcissism fuel suspicion of electoral fraud.
Moderate trust from moderate system justification and authority deference, but undermined by a dangerous worldview, collective narcissism, and populist anti-elitism.
Low trust in parliament (13.64) and very low system justification (7.06) drive mild distrust, though high voting participation (94.66) and strong civic engagement (Active) prevent strong rejection.
